description: Cheryl Gillan served as Secretary of State for Wales from May 2010 to September 2012. She was elected Conservative MP for Chesham and Amersham on 9 April 1992. Education Cheryl was educated at Cheltenham Ladies College and the College of Law. Political career Cheryl was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for the Department for Education and Employment in the last Conservative government. While in opposition, she held several positions including Shadow Minister for Home Affairs and Shadow Secretary of State for Wales. Career outside politics Before entering Parliament, she worked as Director of British Film Year from 1984 to 1986, Senior Marketing Consultant at Ernst and Young from 1986 to 1991 and Marketing Director at Kidsons Impey from 1991 to 1993.

details.body:
Cheryl Gillan served as Secretary of State for Wales from May 2010 to September 2012. She was elected Conservative MP for Chesham and Amersham on 9 April 1992.

Education

Cheryl was educated at Cheltenham Ladies College and the College of Law.

Political career

Cheryl was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for the Department for Education and Employment in the last Conservative government. While in opposition, she held several positions including Shadow Minister for Home Affairs and Shadow Secretary of State for Wales.

Career outside politics

Before entering Parliament, she worked as Director of British Film Year from 1984 to 1986, Senior Marketing Consultant at Ernst and Young from 1986 to 1991 and Marketing Director at Kidsons Impey from 1991 to 1993.

Cheryl Gillan served as Secretary of State for Wales from May 2010 to September 2012. She was elected Conservative MP for Chesham and Amersham on 9 April 1992.

Education

Cheryl was educated at Cheltenham Ladies College and the College of Law.

Political career

Cheryl was Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for the Department for Education and Employment in the last Conservative government. While in opposition, she held several positions including Shadow Minister for Home Affairs and Shadow Secretary of State for Wales.

Career outside politics

Before entering Parliament, she worked as Director of British Film Year from 1984 to 1986, Senior Marketing Consultant at Ernst and Young from 1986 to 1991 and Marketing Director at Kidsons Impey from 1991 to 1993.

The Secretary of State is responsible for the overall strategic direction of the UK Government in Wales.

Responsibilities include:

  • Constitutional and Legislation
  • Economy and Fiscal
  • Steel and TATA Transition Board
  • Industrial Strategy
  • Growth Funds and Economic Zones
  • Energy and Climate Change
  • Culture
  • Brand Wales, Trade & investment
  • Home Affairs and Justice
  • Transport
  • Defence
  • Honours and Public Appointments

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Cheryl Gillan - Secretary of State for Wales
